 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-alo","title":"Improve error messages with actionable suggestions and help links","description":"## Overview
Transform technical error messages into user-friendly diagnostics with context, suggestions, and help resources. Users should be able to self-diagnose and fix issues.

## Dependencies
- **BLOCKED BY**: remote_compilation_helper-nbo (colors) - error display uses colors

## Requirements

### Error Display Format
```
✗ Error: Could not connect to daemon

  The daemon socket was not found at /tmp/rch.sock.
  This usually means the daemon is not running.

  Quick fixes:
    1. Start the daemon: rch daemon start
    2. Check if another instance is running: pgrep rchd
    3. Verify socket path: echo $RCH_SOCKET_PATH

  For more help: https://github.com/your/repo/wiki/Troubleshooting#daemon
```

### Crate Consideration
Consider using `miette` crate for beautiful diagnostics:
- Automatic source code snippets for config errors
- Colored error display with context
- Suggestion system built-in
- OR implement custom display with similar structure

### Error Categories and Messages

#### Connection Errors
| Error | Current Message | Enhanced Message |
|-------|-----------------|------------------|
| Daemon not running | "Daemon socket not found" | Full diagnostic with start command |
| Worker unreachable | "Connection failed" | Include SSH debug hints, host check |
| SSH auth failed | "Permission denied" | Suggest checking key path, permissions |

#### Configuration Errors  
| Error | Current Message | Enhanced Message |
|-------|-----------------|------------------|
| Missing workers.toml | "No workers configured" | Path, creation command, link to docs |
| Invalid TOML | "Parse error" | Line number, expected format, example |
| Invalid value | "confidence_threshold should be..." | Current value, valid range, default |

#### Runtime Errors
| Error | Current Message | Enhanced Message |
|-------|-----------------|------------------|
| rsync failed | "rsync failed with exit code" | Common causes, rsync install check |
| Compilation failed | exit code only | Suggest checking remote logs |

### Implementation

1. Create `rch/src/errors.rs` module:
```rust
pub struct RchError {
    pub kind: ErrorKind,
    pub message: String,
    pub context: Option<String>,
    pub suggestions: Vec<String>,
    pub help_url: Option<String>,
}

impl RchError {
    pub fn display(&self, mode: OutputMode) -> String { ... }
}
```

2. Define ErrorKind enum covering all error types

3. Create constructors for common errors:
```rust
RchError::daemon_not_running(socket_path: &str) -> Self
RchError::worker_unreachable(worker_id: &str, host: &str) -> Self
RchError::config_parse_error(path: &Path, line: usize, msg: &str) -> Self
```

4. Update all error sites to use RchError

### Files to Modify
- Create `rch/src/errors.rs` - error types and display
- `rch/src/commands.rs` - replace anyhow errors with RchError
- `rch/src/hook.rs` - update hook errors
- `rch/src/transfer.rs` - update transfer errors
- `rch/src/config.rs` - update config parsing errors

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
- Test each error type produces correct format
- Test suggestions are appropriate for each error
- Test Plain mode (no colors) error display
- Test JSON mode error serialization

### Integration Tests
- Trigger each error condition and verify message
- Test error chaining (inner errors included)

### E2E Test Additions
```bash
# Scenario: error_messages
# Trigger known error conditions and verify helpful output

# Test daemon not running error
run_scenario "error_daemon_not_running" "verify_error" ""

# Test worker unreachable error  
run_scenario "error_worker_unreachable" "verify_error" "worker-down"

# Test config error
run_scenario "error_invalid_config" "verify_error" "bad-config"
```

### Logging
- Log full error chain at DEBUG level
- Log user-friendly message at ERROR level
- Include timestamp and context for debugging

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All error messages include actionable suggestions
- [ ] Error display is visually clear and easy to scan
- [ ] Common errors have specific, helpful messages
- [ ] JSON mode outputs structured error data
- [ ] Unit tests cover all error types
- [ ] E2E tests verify error scenarios","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T11:36:33.103970136-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T11:58:41.072631042-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-alo","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-nbo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T11:59:05.809708699-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-b9p","title":"Add --json output flag for machine-readable output","description":"## Overview
Add --json flag for machine-readable output, enabling scripting, automation, and integration with other tools (jq, monitoring systems, CI pipelines).

## Dependencies
- **BLOCKED BY**: remote_compilation_helper-u0v (UI output abstraction layer - defines Json OutputMode)

## Requirements

### Global Flag
Add to CLI root:
```rust
#[arg(long, global = true, help = "Output in JSON format")]
json: bool,
```

### Commands Supporting JSON Output

#### `rch status --json`
```json
{
  "daemon": {
    "running": true,
    "socket": "/tmp/rch.sock",
    "uptime_seconds": 3600,
    "pid": 12345
  },
  "workers": {
    "configured": 3,
    "healthy": 2,
    "degraded": 1,
    "unreachable": 0
  },
  "hook": {
    "installed": true,
    "path": "/home/user/.claude/settings.json"
  }
}
```

#### `rch workers list --json`
```json
{
  "workers": [
    {
      "id": "gpu-1",
      "host": "gpu1.internal",
      "user": "ubuntu",
      "status": "healthy",
      "total_slots": 64,
      "available_slots": 32,
      "priority": 100,
      "tags": ["gpu", "fast"]
    }
  ],
  "total": 1
}
```

#### `rch workers probe --json`
```json
{
  "results": [
    {
      "worker_id": "gpu-1",
      "success": true,
      "latency_ms": 45,
      "error": null
    }
  ]
}
```

#### `rch config show --json`
```json
{
  "general": {
    "enabled": true,
    "log_level": "info",
    "socket_path": "/tmp/rch.sock"
  },
  "compilation": {
    "confidence_threshold": 0.85,
    "min_local_time_ms": 2000
  },
  "transfer": {
    "compression_level": 3,
    "exclude_patterns": ["target/", ".git/"]
  }
}
```

#### `rch config validate --json`
```json
{
  "valid": true,
  "errors": [],
  "warnings": [
    {
      "file": "config.toml",
      "field": "compression_level", 
      "message": "Value 20 exceeds recommended maximum of 19"
    }
  ]
}
```

#### `rch daemon status --json`
```json
{
  "running": true,
  "socket": "/tmp/rch.sock",
  "pid": 12345,
  "uptime_seconds": 3600
}
```

### Implementation

1. Add Serialize derives to relevant structs in types.rs
2. Create JSON output structs in ui.rs for commands without existing types
3. In OutputContext, add:
```rust
pub fn output_json<T: Serialize>(&self, value: &T) {
    if self.mode == OutputMode::Json {
        println!("{}", serde_json::to_string_pretty(value).unwrap());
    }
}
```
4. Update each command to check mode and output JSON

### Behavior Rules
- When --json is specified, ONLY output JSON (no headers, no human text)
- Errors in JSON mode should also be JSON: `{"error": "message", "code": "ERROR_CODE"}`
- Pretty-print by default, add --json-compact for minified output (future)

### Files to Modify
- `rch/src/main.rs` - add --json global flag
- `rch/src/ui.rs` - add JSON output method
- `rch/src/commands.rs` - update all commands with JSON branches
- `rch-common/src/types.rs` - add Serialize to types

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
- Test JSON output is valid JSON (parse it back)
- Test JSON contains all expected fields
- Test JSON error format

### Integration Tests
- Pipe JSON output to jq and verify it parses
- Test each command's JSON schema

### E2E Test Additions
```bash
# Scenario: json_output
# Verify JSON output is parseable

test_json_output() {
    local cmd="$1"
    local output
    output=$($cmd --json 2>&1)
    if ! echo "$output" | jq . > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        log "FAIL" "JSON" "$cmd produced invalid JSON"
        return 1
    fi
    log "INFO" "JSON" "$cmd JSON valid"
}

test_json_output "rch status"
test_json_output "rch workers list"
test_json_output "rch config show"
```

### Schema Validation (Optional Future Enhancement)
- Define JSON schemas for each command
- Validate output against schema in tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] --json flag works globally
- [ ] All listed commands support JSON output
- [ ] JSON is valid and parseable
- [ ] Error output in JSON mode is also JSON
- [ ] Unit tests verify JSON structure
- [ ] E2E tests verify JSON is parseable by jq","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T11:37:03.672228669-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T11:59:08.236039485-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-b9p","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-u0v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T11:59:35.192422114-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

+{"id":"remote_compilation_helper-btf","title":"Add interactive config initialization wizard","description":"## Overview
Transform config initialization from silent file creation into an interactive wizard that guides users through setup with validation and helpful prompts.

## Dependencies
- **BLOCKED BY**: remote_compilation_helper-nbo (colors) - wizard uses colored prompts
- **BLOCKED BY**: remote_compilation_helper-cmj (status indicators) - uses indicators for validation

## Requirements

### Crate Selection
Use `dialoguer` crate for interactive prompts:
- Input fields with validation
- Confirmation prompts
- Select lists
- Theme customization

### Interactive Flow

```
$ rch config init

╭─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│  RCH Configuration Wizard                                   │
│  Let's set up your remote compilation workers.              │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

📍 Step 1/4: Worker Configuration

? Worker ID (unique name for this worker): gpu-worker
? SSH hostname or IP: gpu.internal.company.com
? SSH username: ubuntu
? SSH identity file path: ~/.ssh/id_rsa

  Validating connection...
  ✓ SSH connection successful (45ms)

? Add another worker? (y/N): n

📍 Step 2/4: Compilation Settings

? Minimum local compile time to intercept (ms) [2000]:
? Confidence threshold (0.0-1.0) [0.85]:

📍 Step 3/4: Transfer Settings

? Compression level (1-19, higher = slower but smaller) [3]:
? Additional exclude patterns (comma-separated, empty for defaults):

📍 Step 4/4: Confirmation

Configuration Summary:
  Workers:     1 configured (gpu-worker)
  Threshold:   2000ms / 85% confidence
  Compression: Level 3

? Write configuration? (Y/n): y

✓ Created: ~/.config/rch/config.toml
✓ Created: ~/.config/rch/workers.toml

Next steps:
  1. Start the daemon:  rch daemon start
  2. Install the hook:  rch hook install
  3. Check status:      rch status
```

### Non-Interactive Mode
For CI/scripting, support flags:
```bash
rch config init --non-interactive \
  --worker-id=gpu-1 \
  --worker-host=gpu.internal \
  --worker-user=ubuntu \
  --worker-key=~/.ssh/id_rsa
```

Or accept a config file:
```bash
rch config init --from-file=my-workers.toml
```

### Validation During Input
- Worker ID: non-empty, valid characters
- Hostname: valid hostname/IP format
- SSH key: file exists, proper permissions
- Confidence: 0.0-1.0 range
- Compression: 1-19 range

### Optional: Connection Test
After entering worker details, offer to test:
```
? Test SSH connection to gpu-worker? (Y/n): y
  Connecting to gpu.internal...
  ✓ Connection successful (45ms)
  ✓ rsync available on remote
```

### Files to Modify
- `rch/src/commands.rs` - expand config_init() function
- `rch/src/main.rs` - add flags for non-interactive mode
- `Cargo.toml` (rch) - add dialoguer dependency

### Code Structure
```rust
pub async fn config_init(
    non_interactive: bool,
    worker_id: Option<String>,
    worker_host: Option<String>,
    worker_user: Option<String>,
    worker_key: Option<String>,
) -> Result<()> {
    if non_interactive {
        return config_init_non_interactive(...);
    }
    config_init_interactive().await
}

async fn config_init_interactive() -> Result<()> {
    // Use dialoguer for prompts
    let worker_id: String = Input::with_theme(&theme())
        .with_prompt("Worker ID")
        .validate_with(|input: &String| {
            if input.is_empty() {
                Err("Worker ID cannot be empty")
            } else {
                Ok(())
            }
        })
        .interact_text()?;

    // ... continue with other prompts
}
```

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
- Test validation functions (hostname, range checks, etc.)
- Test config file generation from input
- Test non-interactive mode with various flag combinations

### Integration Tests
- Test full interactive flow with mocked input
- Test non-interactive mode creates valid config
- Test validation rejects invalid input

### E2E Test Additions
```bash
# Test non-interactive mode
test_config_init_non_interactive() {
    local temp_dir
    temp_dir=$(mktemp -d)

    # Run non-interactive init
    RCH_CONFIG_DIR="$temp_dir" rch config init \
        --non-interactive \
        --worker-id=test-worker \
        --worker-host=test.host \
        --worker-user=testuser \
        --worker-key=~/.ssh/id_rsa

    # Verify files created
    [[ -f "$temp_dir/workers.toml" ]] || die "workers.toml not created"

    # Verify content
    grep -q "test-worker" "$temp_dir/workers.toml" || die "Worker ID not in config"

    log "INFO" "CONFIG" "Non-interactive init OK"
    # Clean up temp directory after test
}
```

### Manual Testing
- [ ] Run full interactive wizard
- [ ] Test each validation message
- [ ] Test connection testing feature
- [ ] Test cancellation (Ctrl+C) at each step
- [ ] Test non-interactive with all flags
- [ ] Test with missing required flags (should error clearly)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Interactive wizard guides user through all settings
- [ ] Input validation with helpful error messages
- [ ] Optional connection testing
- [ ] Non-interactive mode for automation
- [ ] Creates valid configuration files
- [ ] Colored output matching theme
- [ ] Unit tests for validation logic
- [ ] E2E tests for non-interactive mode
- [ ] Graceful Ctrl+C handling","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T11:37:17.463952233-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T12:02:36.752193682-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-btf","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-nbo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T12:02:43.264255984-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-btf","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-cmj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T12:02:43.359005211-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-ceb","title":"Bug: Daemon health checks don't respect RCH_MOCK_SSH mode","description":"When RCH_MOCK_SSH=1 is set, the daemon's health check still tries to make real SSH connections, causing mock workers to be marked unhealthy. This breaks E2E tests in mock mode.  {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-cmj","title":"Standardize status indicators (✓/✗/⚠) across all commands","description":"## Overview
Standardize status indicator symbols and their meanings across ALL commands. Ensure visual consistency and immediate recognizability.

## Dependencies
- **BLOCKED BY**: remote_compilation_helper-nbo (colors) - indicators need color support

## Requirements

### Standard Status Indicators
Define enum in ui.rs:
```rust
pub enum StatusIndicator {
    Success,    // ✓ (green) - operation succeeded, healthy state
    Error,      // ✗ (red) - operation failed, error state
    Warning,    // ⚠ (yellow) - degraded, needs attention
    Info,       // ● (cyan) - neutral information
    Pending,    // ○ (gray) - waiting, not started
    InProgress, // ◐ (blue) - currently running
    Disabled,   // ⊘ (gray) - intentionally disabled
}
```

### Application Mapping

| Context | Current | Should Be |
|---------|---------|-----------|
| Worker healthy | "OK" or "✓" | ✓ (green) |
| Worker unreachable | "✗" | ✗ (red) |
| Worker degraded | varies | ⚠ (yellow) |
| Worker disabled | plain text | ⊘ (gray) |
| Daemon running | "Status: Running" | ✓ Running (green) |
| Daemon stopped | "Status: Not running" | ✗ Stopped (red) |
| Config valid | "✓" | ✓ Valid (green) |
| Config warning | "⚠" | ⚠ with explanation (yellow) |
| Config error | "✗" | ✗ with explanation (red) |
| Hook installed | plain text | ✓ Installed (green) |
| Hook not installed | plain text | ○ Not installed (gray) |
| Probe success | "✓ OK (100ms)" | ✓ 100ms (green) |
| Probe failed | "✗ Error: ..." | ✗ Error message (red) |

### Implementation
1. Create `StatusIndicator::display(&self, mode: OutputMode) -> String` method
2. Update ALL status displays in commands.rs to use StatusIndicator
3. Ensure consistent spacing after indicators

### Files to Modify
- `rch/src/ui.rs` - add StatusIndicator enum and display logic
- `rch/src/commands.rs` - update all status displays (lines 176, 179, 182, 188, 228, 231, 234, 240, 312-327, 629-687, 696-703, 873-908)

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
- Test each StatusIndicator produces correct symbol and color
- Test Plain mode produces symbols without ANSI codes
- Test JSON mode produces structured status

### Integration Tests
- Snapshot tests for status command output
- Verify all status displays use the standard indicators

### E2E Test Additions
```bash
# Scenario: status_indicators
# Verify consistent indicators across commands
run_scenario "status_consistency" "verify" ""
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All commands use StatusIndicator enum
- [ ] No hardcoded status symbols remain in commands.rs
- [ ] Visual consistency verified across all commands
- [ ] Unit tests cover all indicator types
- [ ] Snapshot tests capture expected output format","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T11:36:34.370314322-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T11:57:46.648417643-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-cmj","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-nbo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T11:58:14.488091447-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-ei5","title":"RCH Core TODOs Master Plan (Hook + WorkerPool + CLI + Tests)","description":"Background
- RCH is a transparent compilation offloading system; the hook must be fast, precise, and fail-open.
- The current codebase has core scaffolding; this plan captures the remaining high-leverage TODOs in a self-contained way.

Scope
- Hook integration (classification → daemon → transfer pipeline → artifacts)
- WorkerPool correctness (counting, status, health recovery)
- rch CLI commands (daemon/workers/status/config/hook) with clear UX
- Comprehensive tests (unit/integration/e2e) + detailed logging

Non-Goals
- New features beyond the above TODOs (e.g., UI, metrics, autoscaling)

Principles
- Fail-open: errors in remote pipeline must allow local execution.
- Precision over recall for classification; correctness over cleverness.
- Observability: log enough to debug without overwhelming normal output.","design":"This master epic decomposes three top TODO areas into actionable, dependency-aware tasks, plus a testing epic. The structure allows parallel work while preserving ordering constraints (e.g., CLI tests depend on CLI implementations). Each task includes background, goal, and acceptance to minimize future ambiguity.","acceptance_criteria":"- All child epics are created, linked, and contain granular tasks with dependencies.
- Each task contains enough context to implement without re-reading the long plan document.
- Test tasks explicitly cover unit, integration, and e2e with logging expectations.","notes":"If any task is already implemented in HEAD, verify by code inspection + tests, then close with a note referencing evidence.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T09:13:18.056378843-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T10:42:09.690778309-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-16T10:42:09.690778309-05:00","close_reason":"All child epics completed: Hook pipeline, WorkerPool, CLI commands, and Testing/E2E coverage"}
